analysis.core_story_summary: One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Battle Between Jalandhar & The Deities — Kids (8-12):** One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu. This story teaches the cost of conflict. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Battle Between Jalandhar & The Deities — Teens (13-17):** One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu. For teens, the key idea is the cost of conflict. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **Battle Between Jalandhar & The Deities — Young Adults:** One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu. A modern takeaway is the cost of conflict. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Battle Between Jalandhar & The Deities — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu. The episode highlights the cost of conflict. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Battle Between Jalandhar & The Deities — Global General Audience:** One day Sage Bhrigu came to meet Jalandhar. After receiving him with due respect Jalandhar asked him as to who severed the head of Rahu. Sage Bhrigu then told him about Hiranyakashipu who was the maternal uncle of Rahu. In universal terms, it explores the cost of conflict. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
